key_insight: The paper replaces thresholding on noisy raw nonlinear residuals with online-learned residual structure to more reliably identify which variables should be eliminated in nonlinear elimination preconditioning.
engineering_relevance: This improves robustness and scalability of nonlinear solvers for large PDE systems by making preconditioner selection less sensitive to hand-tuned parameters and local oscillations.
method: Collect residual snapshots during Newton stagnation phases.
Train an unsupervised extractor (PCA or autoencoder) to learn the dominant residual pattern.
Use the reconstructed residual to define the bad subset, within a parallel domain-decomposition framework.
evidence_signal: The method was evaluated on lid-driven cavity flow problems up to Reynolds number 10,000 and reported more coherent bad subsets, better robustness to parameters, and improved convergence over the baseline NE preconditioner.
watch_item: The approach adds online training overhead and may depend on the quality and timing of stagnation-phase snapshots on each subdomain.
paper_title: A domain decomposition online-learning-enhanced nonlinear elimination preconditioner
